title: This Boston hotel is giving music lovers a reason to travel
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Omni Boston Hotel at the Seaport launched the All Access Pass package, including sold-out concert tickets.
Concert tourism in Boston is rising, with high demand for events featuring BTS, Noah Kahan, and Ariana Grande.
Logan Airport introduced a biometric system allowing TSA PreCheck flyers to verify identity with facial recognition.

title: Tom Brady reveals that he has ‘explored a lot of different things’ about making an NFL comeback
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Tom Brady explored NFL rules for a comeback post-retirement, which would require divesting his Raiders' ownership stake.
The NFL's policy prohibits active players owning team stakes, complicating any return for Brady as a player/owner.
Brady, 48, has embraced roles like broadcasting and Raiders' minority ownership, maintaining ties to football post-retirement.

title: Police release bodycam footage of Plymouth butcher knife standoff
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Plymouth police released bodycam footage of a standoff involving a man holding his wife at knifepoint.
The suspect reportedly broke into the home on Clark Road, barricaded himself, and held a large knife to the woman's neck.
Officers negotiated for 45 minutes before the suspect surrendered; four children were safely removed from the home.

title: MLB debuts automated strike challenge system for 2026 season. Here’s what you need to know.
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: MLB introduces Automated Ball-Strike Challenge System for the 2026 season to improve accuracy and add strategy to the game.
Players can challenge umpire's ball or strike calls in real time, with the system using T-Mobile's 5G network for quick results.
Each team gets two challenges, with successful ones retained; the system isn't used in special event games like the Field of Dreams.

title: Kelsey Fitzsimmons acquitted of assault charge
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Kelsey Fitzsimmons was acquitted of assault with a dangerous weapon on March 26, after a bench trial in North Andover.
Judge Jeffrey Karp ruled the prosecution didn't prove beyond a reasonable doubt that Fitzsimmons aimed a gun at Officer Patrick Noonan.
The incident occurred during a June 30, 2025, confrontation, where Fitzsimmons claimed she attempted suicide, while Noonan shot her.

title: OKC’s Shai Gilgeous-Alexander impressed by ‘playoff feel’ in loss to Celtics
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Celtics defeated the Thunder at TD Garden, securing a half-game lead over the Knicks for second place in the East.
Shai Gilgeous-Alexander praised the Celtics' atmosphere, describing the game as having a 'playoff feel' despite not being nationally televised.
The Celtics and Thunder split their season series, with each team winning one game, culminating in a potential Finals rematch.

title: Trooper arrested in Boston for OUI was allegedly ‘slumped’ at the wheel with High Noon in cupholder
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Trooper Donovan Preston, 31, was arrested for OUI in Boston after being found slumped at the wheel with a High Noon.
Preston pleaded not guilty to OUI and open container charges; his car was towed and license seized by police.
Preston has been suspended by State Police pending internal discipline as his criminal case proceeds, with a court date set for May 13.

title: What you need to know about Brazil vs. France at Gillette Stadium
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Brazil and France will play a friendly match at Gillette Stadium, kicking off at 4 p.m. Thursday.
The game will be broadcast on ESPN2, ESPN Deportes, and streamed on ESPN+; tickets start at $230 on Ticketmaster.
The match serves as a pre-World Cup trial for logistics and player evaluations, including special MBTA train service.

title: Closing arguments delivered in trial of Kelsey Fitzsimmons. Here’s what happened.
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Kelsey Fitzsimmons, a former North Andover police officer, is on trial for assault and battery with a dangerous weapon charges.
Fitzsimmons was shot by Officer Patrick Noonan in June 2025 during a restraining order execution at her home.
The prosecution argued Fitzsimmons lied about not aiming a gun, while the defense claimed Noonan overreacted.

title: 17-year-old killed in Brockton shooting, second fatal shooting in the city this week
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A 17-year-old named Jeffry Damestoire was fatally shot in Brockton, marking the second fatal shooting in the city this week.
18-year-old Tyree St. Cloud pleaded not guilty to murder charges; authorities allege he shot Damestoire after a physical altercation.
St. Cloud is held pending further proceedings and is scheduled to appear in court again on April 13.
